name: Log4Shell Evasion Pattern
notes: This rule triggers when potential Remote Code Execution related to Log4Shell Exploit (CVE-2021-44228) is observed. This could indicate an attacker bypassing it via techniques such as system environment variables, lower or upper lookup, invalid unicode characters with upper, system properties, html url encoding and notations.
db_fields_used: URL, URL Host, URL Referrer, Request URI
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Apache HTTP Server, Amazon AWS WAF, Microsoft IIS, NGINX HTTP Server, Amazon AWS Elastic Kubernetes Service, F5 Networks BIG-IP AFM
when any of URL (custom), URL Host (custom), URL Referrer (custom), Request URI (custom) match (?i)(\$|%24)(\{|%7[bB])(lower|upper|env|::-\w+|base64)
TD_list: APPLY Log4Shell Evasion Pattern on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Apache HTTP Server, Amazon AWS WAF, Microsoft IIS, NGINX HTTP Server, Amazon AWS Elastic Kubernetes Service, F5 Networks BIG-IP AFM
AND when any of URL (custom), URL Host (custom), URL Referrer (custom), Request URI (custom) match (?i)(\$|%24)(\{|%7[bB])(lower|upper|env|::-\w+|base64)
RED_list: Event name: Potential Log4Shell Evasion detected
Event description: An event was found that could be associated with Log4Shell CVE-2021-44228
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
